summary: A small two-story rectangular Minecraft house with a raised stone foundation, repeating horizontal banding of materials, narrow horizontal windows on both floors, a stepped gable-like wooden roof, and a low white border/planter surrounding the footprint.

shape.overall_form: rectangular block with slightly tapered/stepped roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along long axis (mirrored front/back and left/right on seen facades)

materials.name: wood
materials.role: roof and horizontal trim (likely oak planks)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: stone slab trim on roof edges and band

elements: two stacked floors with continuous narrow windows on each floor (long horizontal rows)
horizontal material bands: stonebrick at bottom, white band mid, wood trim between bands
wooden stepped/gabled roof with slab/step accents at peak
white low border around building (1 block high) forming a recessed planter area
rebuild_hints: Footprint: an 11x5 block rectangular main volume (estimate), centered inside a 13x7 white border; adjust if needed from images.

Walls: repeat pattern vertically: bottom 2 rows stonebrick with windows inset, then narrow wood trim row, then 1-2 row white (quartz) band, then stonebrick row with windows, capped by wood trim and roof.
Corners: use cobblestone full blocks at vertical corners extending full height of visible stone bands.
Windows: narrow 1-block-high glass strips; place them in continuous rows along long sides with 1-block gaps between window groups as seen.
Roof: construct a symmetrical gable with oak planks using stairs/slabs to create stepped profile and small central peak detail; cap ends with stone slab accents.
uncertainties: Exact footprint dimensions (width/depth) are estimated from pixel views and top-down images; could be off by ±1-2 blocks.
Material IDs: white blocks appear to be quartz but could be smooth stone or concrete; stone slab and exact slab material ambiguous.
Some small trim blocks (slabs/stairs) on roof and band edges are hard to distinguish precisely (stone vs. smooth stone vs. stonebrick slabs).
Internal floor heights and interior layout are not visible; number of internal floors assumed from exterior banding.
